title: Home & Family
shortSummary: Talk about your family members and describe where you live.
culturalNote: Family ties in Germany are warm but often more independent than in some cultures: young adults frequently move out for study or work, yet stay closely connected by phone and regular visits. Renting is the norm rather than buying, even for life, and a tidy, well-kept home is a point of quiet pride. The cosy feeling of a comfortable home has its own word: Gemütlichkeit.

grammar.explanation: The verb haben (to have) takes an accusative object, so masculine articles change: 'Ich habe einen Bruder' (einen, not ein). Feminine and neuter stay the same.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying 'Ich habe ein Bruder'. The masculine object must be 'einen Bruder'.

grammar.explanation: Possessives match the family member's gender: mein Vater (m), meine Mutter (f), mein Kind (n), meine Eltern (plural). The owner does not change the ending; the owned noun does.
grammar.commonMistake: Matching the possessive to yourself rather than to the family member's gender.

dialogue.setup: Paul shows his friend Nina a family photo on his phone.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.german: Ja. Das sind meine Eltern und meine Schwester.
lines.english: Yes. Those are my parents and my sister.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.english: Do you also have a brother?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.german: Nein, nur eine Schwester. Aber wir haben einen Hund.
lines.english: No, just a sister. But we have a dog.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.german: Süß! Wohnt deine Schwester noch zu Hause?
lines.english: Cute! Does your sister still live at home?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.german: Nein, sie wohnt in einer Wohnung in München.
lines.english: No, she lives in a flat in Munich.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I have a brother'?
quiz.explanation: haben takes the accusative, so masculine Bruder needs 'einen'.

quiz.question: Which means 'my sister'?
quiz.explanation: Schwester is feminine, so the possessive is 'meine'.

quiz.question: Hast du _____? (Do you have siblings?)
quiz.explanation: Geschwister is the word for siblings.

quiz.question: Arrange: meine / das / Familie / ist
quiz.explanation: Das ist (this is) plus the possessive phrase.
